title: Bi-weekly history test answer to worlds problems

enrichment.summary: Kyle proposes a 'biweekly history test' as his candidate for the best invention ever: every two weeks, use a random number generator to pick decades, spend one minute per decade talking about it for an hour, grade yourself, and post results publicly on YouTube to build historical knowledge and accountability. He argues history is the best general education because it reveals what went right and wrong, and he challenges himself and others to adopt this habit.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Studying history is the best general education because it lets you understand what went right and wrong and make better decisions now
His best invention ever and the best mechanism to force society to become educated
Culture conflates money-making skills with real intelligence; we should distinguish proper education from 'mental masturbation'
People get too stuck on one subject and need to diversify their historical knowledge
Everyone should make educational videos regardless of polish; enthusiasm for the lifestyle matters more than production quality
Thinks it's actually a good idea to demonstrate the test is accessible to everyone, not just a select few

entities: Gibbon's Decline and Fall of Rome

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle doesn't usually talk in videos because he figures it out on his own and doesn't have time to clean up his room.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes this video is important because it discusses the best invention ever.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's best invention ever is a call to action for everyone in the world to make the world a better place, inspired by Michael Jackson's song 'Man in the Mirror.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's invention is a biweekly, hour-long history test where participants use a random number generator to select a decade and talk about it for one minute.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Participants grade themselves based on what they know about the selected decade.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes this history test is beneficial because it motivates people to study history and realize how little they know.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks this history test is a perfect way to challenge people to share what they know and force society to become educated.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle suggests that participants can use a video camera to film themselves taking the test and post their grades on their website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes this history test is the cheapest and best way to prove how knowledgeable people are about history.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to take the history test himself to motivate himself to diversify his knowledge of history.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks the problem with education is that people don't diversify their knowledge and get stuck on one subject.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle recommends studying history by listening to audiobooks while writing or biking.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes studying history is the best way to educate oneself because it helps understand what went right and wrong in the past.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks it's important to show people that making educational videos is for everyone, not just a few people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle compares making historical videos to early attempts at flying across the ocean, suggesting it will become easier in the future.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to create a page on his website to record and judge the history test scores every two weeks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes participants can quickly judge their own videos because they remember what they said.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle suggests that participants can use a timeline on his website to check what they missed and grade themselves.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks that eventually, third parties will step in to judge the history test videos and standardize the grading.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is currently drawing a region of Rome and wants to finish it completely to see what it looks like.
